input.background: In a study of maternal cigarette smoking and bone density in newborns, 77 infants of mothers who smoked had a mean bone mineral content of 0.098 g/cm^3(s1=0.026 g/cm^3). The 161 infants whose mothers did not smoke had a mean bone mineral content of 0.095 g/cm^3(s2=0.025 g/cm^3).
input.question: Calculate the 95% confidence interval for μ1−μ2.

mean_smoking.description: Mean bone mineral content (g/cm^3) for infants of mothers who smoked.

sd_smoking.description: Standard deviation of bone mineral content (g/cm^3) for infants of mothers who smoked.

n_smoking.description: Sample size of infants whose mothers smoked.

mean_non_smoking.description: Mean bone mineral content (g/cm^3) for infants of mothers who did not smoke.

sd_non_smoking.description: Standard deviation of bone mineral content (g/cm^3) for infants of mothers who did not smoke.

n_non_smoking.description: Sample size of infants whose mothers did not smoke.

mean_difference.description: Difference in mean bone mineral content (μ1−μ2) between infants of mothers who smoked and those who did not smoke.

confidence_interval.description: 95% confidence interval for the difference in mean bone mineral content (μ1−μ2).
